verse_text: Now behold, there was a man named Joseph, acouncil member, good and just man.

links.context: Joseph of Arimathea was a hidden disciple, one who believed in Jesus but had made no public acknowledgement of faith. Nicodemus had come to Jesus by night (3:2) and later defended Him before the Pharisees (7:50-52). By burying Him, both show their devotion to our Lord publicly, in stark contrast to the fearful disciples, who had scattered. See also note at [[LUK.23:50]]-53.
